error_signature: Access to the file is not allowed
title: Access Denied Error for Mounted Volume in n8n
symptom: When using the 'Watch Directory' trigger, n8n throws 'Access to the file is not allowed' when trying to read files. This occurs intermittently and prevents the workflow from processing PDF files.
applies_when: Files are located in a mounted volume
not_this_if: Files are accessed through a different method (e.g., 'Schedule Trigger')
The volume is not mounted correctly
root_cause: The error occurs because the default environment variable `N8N_BLOCK_FILE_ACCESS_TO_N8N_FILES` is set to true, which restricts access to files within the n8n directory. This can lead to permission issues when trying to access files in mounted volumes that are mapped to paths within the n8n directory.
fix_steps: Change the volume mapping in your docker-compose.yml to a path outside of the n8n directory, e.g., '/volume1/knowledge_base/research_data:/research_data'.
Restart the n8n container to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Update the volume mapping in your Docker configuration to avoid using paths within the n8n directory.
